chunks.text: Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional & Electronics Support Group /share/b/pub/lab-temp.help Mar 17, 2003 The temperature in the EECS Instructional labs in Cory and Soda Halls is controlled by equipment

in Cory and Soda Halls is controlled by equipment that is maintained by the central campus Office of Facilities Management. The equipment does not react quickly to changes in the temperature outside or to crowding in one lab or another. So there ar

or to crowding in one lab or another. So there are times when the temperatures in the labs are not ideal. If you find that the temperature remains too hot or cold for more than a day, you can report it by email to "inst@eecs". All that we can do i

it by email to "inst@eecs". All that we can do is report it to Facilities Management. They may not respond immediately (and may take several *days* to respond) so please be patient. Instructional Support Group 378/384/386 Cory Hall, 333
onal Support Group 378/384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
